title: Vehicles abandoned on streets of Bengaluru being removed by GBA, BTP

content: Dubbed ‘Mission Aagaman’ (meaning arrival), the successful launch of Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 marks India’s entry into an elite group of nations with private orbital launch capability

title: 2,914 students dropped out of State syllabus schools in 2024-25 in Kerala; 36.65% are migrant workers’ children
content: They discontinued studies either because they, along with their parents, had returned to their native States or their parents had shifted to another location for work, Minister for General Education N. Samsudheen said in a written reply in Assembly recently

title: Wangchuk’s removal from Jantar Mantar: Congress accuses govt of crushing peaceful protest
content: Attacking the BJP, the Opposition party also said it was a shame that the world's largest democracy was being "ruled" by the most undemocratic and anti-democratic political party in the world

title: Gujarat tops NITI Aayog’s first Investment Friendliness Index 2026 among major States
content: Gujarat scored 56.6 points, ahead of Maharashtra (53.7) and Tamil Nadu (53.3), in an assessment of 17 major States

title: M.A. Baby condemns removal of activist Sonam Wangchuk from Jantar Mantar protest site
content: Criticising ‘the authoritarian attitude’ of the Modi government, CPI(M) general secretary M.A. Baby said that ‘silencing dissent cannot be a substitute for accountability’

title: Sharad Pawar slams Centre over ‘irresponsible’ handling of Wangchuk's hunger strike
content: Mr. Pawar asserted that while the government moved to hospitalise Mr. Wangchuk as the situation became "uncontrollable", the protest would continue regardless of the action against the activist

content: The Bench had also issued notice to the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust seeking its response in the matter

title: CJP’s Abhijeet Dipke begins indefinite hunger strike, as police shifts Wangchuk to hospital
content: Mr. Dipke’s announcement came as the New Delhi DCP’s office said that Mr. Wangchuk has been shifted to the hospital for ‘essential medical care’

content: Society to Save Rocks first exposed the destruction of rocks. The Hindu  reported encroachment of land near Ammuguda and destruction of rock formations which were billions of years old

title: Kejriwal slams Centre over Sonam Wangchuk’s hospitalisation, calls it ‘arrogance’
content: Instead of forcibly lifting him (Mr. Wangchuk) up, the Modi government should have talked to Sonam Wangchuk, says AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal

title: IMA plans protest against assault on doctors in Dombivli on July 20
content: The IMA has urged government to take strict action against all those involved in the assault, without any political interference and to amend the Maharashtra Medicare Act to prevent violence against healthcare workers.

title: Rescuers race to find survivors in rain after landslip in China kills at least eight
content: The landslide contained about 18,000 cubic meters of rocks and debris, and the largest single rock was around 3,000 cubic meters, Wang Chuanjun, head of Planning and Natural Resources in Pengshui County, told a news conference on July 17

title: Why Kerala’s two private airline projects have hit an air pocket
content: Though Air Kerala and Alhind Air were expected to begin operations last year, uncertainty has derailed their plans, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions and investment woes

title: 23 Andhra villages secure ISO certification for quality citizen services, administration
content: Deputy CM and Minister for Rural Development K. Pawan Kalyan said the international certification was a testament to the good governance in villages

title: Coastal lions depend mainly on wild prey, study finds
content: Among the prey species, blue bull (nilgai) was the largest contributor, accounting for 51% of the biomass consumed by lions, wild pigs were the second most important wild prey, while cattle formed the largest domestic prey component

title: ‘Sharad Pawar should take the final call on allying with NDA’
content: Merger unlikely, say leaders from NCP and NCP(SP); Eknath Shinde stays out, as anxiety in NCP(SP) camp grows

title: SIR schedule revised for Maharashtra, extended by 10 days
content: As per the revised schedule, the draft voter list will be published on August 17, and the final list will be published on October 19

content: The remarks came after his interaction with Prime Minister Modi after an official programme in Chandigarh garnered attention on social media

content: Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) posted on X that Mr. Wangchuk’s wife, Gitanjali Angmo, was not allowed to carry a phone to meet him in the emergency ward

title: Ram temple donation theft: Police take custody of Tinnu, nephew Manish Yadav
content: On Friday a special anti-corruption court granted a 39-hour police remand of the two key accused, though the police had sought seven days said officials

title: Nothing should be administered to Sonam Wangchuk without my consent, says wife Gitanjali Angmo
content: The Delhi Police has shifted activist Sonam Wangchuk to the Safdarjung Hospital, his wife Gitanjali J Angmo said on Saturday, July 18

title: FIFA weekend puts Hyderabad’s nightlife to test as hospitality industry eyes bigger goal
content: NRAI issues an advisory to restaurants, bars, clubs and microbreweries to ensure the two nights pass without incident

title: Support swells for Sonam Wangchuk’s indefinite fast
content: Several civil society organisations express solidarity with educationist and environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk, who is on an indefinite hunger strike in New Delhi

title: EXIM operations at Kerala’s Vizhinjam seaport set to begin on August 18
content: According to a statement issued by Office of Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan, commencement of EXIM operations will transform Vizhinjam from a transshipment port into a full-fledged international cargo gateway

title: At least five injured in blast at sponge iron factory in West Bengal
content: Another police officer said the temperature around the furnace shot up to several degrees Celsius after the blast, making rescue operations extremely difficult

title: Groping breasts not part of routine gynaecological examination, says Gauhati High Court
content: Considering the convicted doctor’s age, the court reduces his sentence in the 17-year-old case to ₹5,000 in default of imprisonment for two months

title: Minor boy opens fire at 16-year-old ‘girlfriend’ in Bengaluru, buries body with family’s help
content: For now, the police have recorded statements from both families but have identified several loose ends. The investigation will continue to ascertain whether there was negligence on the part of multiple people in the case

title: Sai Krishna custodial death: What happened and why is the Andhra government in the dock? | Explained
content: The alleged custodial death of Sai Krishna has triggered political outrage in Andhra, with claims of torture, evidence tampering and a cover-up. A look at the case and the investigation so far

title: All you need to know about Vikram-1, India’s first privately developed orbital-class rocket
content: Vikram-1 is a seven-storey-tall, multi-stage orbital launch vehicle built with an all-carbon composite structure and powered by in-house developed propulsion systems

title: Sonam Wangchuk shifted to Safdarjung Hospital on 21st day of hunger strike
content: The New Delhi DCP office said that Sonam Wangchuk has been shifted to the hospital as per the orders of the Delhi High Court and on expert medical advice due to his deteriorating health condition

title: Students lead plantation drive as Bidar Parisara Vahini takes environment campaign to schools
content: The programme was part of Bidar Parisara Vahini’s initiative to take environmental awareness campaigns to schools across the Kalyana Karnataka region

title: Kerala looks to the skies to tackle human-wildlife conflict, but is this feasible?
content: Kerala Forest Department has proposed using Indian Air Force helicopters to relocate captured Schedule I animals, including tigers and leopards, from conflict hotspots to secure forest habitats. However, the move has sparked debate over its ecological and economic feasibility

title: India rejects former Japan Minister’s remarks on Shinkansen project delay
content: The clarification comes in response to a recent critical social media post by a former Japanese Minister regarding the project, which will be India’s first bullet train network.

title: Taj Mahal or ‘Tejo Mahalaya’? The legal tussle explained
content: In 2017, the Archaeological Survey of India stated unambiguously that the Taj was a 17th-century tomb. The technology used in its construction and design, including pietra dura, did not exist in pre-medieval days. It should have ended any vestiges of controversy.

title: Supreme Court to examine plea for recognition of intersex persons as a distinct class
content: Petitioner seeks nationwide ban on medically unnecessary genital surgeries on intersex children; calls for provision of identity documents and job reservation

title: ₹10,000-crore investment claim: Tata’s denial embarrasses Kerala CM
content: PRD statement says Chief Minister was referring to ₹10,000-crore investment potential that the government aims at attracting to the State over the coming years under the maritime initiative

title: Factional equations, community balance shape Congress’ deputy mayor choice in Kochi
content: The post fell vacant after incumbent Deepak Joy resigned on May 13, shortly after being elected as Thripunithura MLA

title: ITC hotels announces acquisition of 130 key operating hotel in Ahmedabad’s commercial hub
content: The addition of this hotel would further strengthen the company’s owned asset portfolio in Ahmedabad by straddling across all the branded hospitality market segments and price points, the company said.

title: Opposition support grows as Wangchuk’s hunger strike continues on day 20
content: The protest seeking Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation attracted more high-profile visitors

title: India’s first private orbital rocket Vikram-1 set for lift-off
content: Vikram-1’s flight sequence, from lift-off to orbit injection, will cover 14 phases, and the total duration of the flight will be 15.46 minutes

title: The Krishna Janmabhoomi and Shahi Idgah timeline: History, compromise & legal battle
content: Through the centuries, the Krishna Janmabhoomi temple has been ‘reclaimed’ several times before a compromise agreement was struck with the Shahi Idgah mosque. This is now being challenged.

title: Krishna Janmabhoomi-Shahi Eidgah dispute: court relies upon government notification of 1920 that indicates existence of pre-Aurangzeb temple
content: Religious character of the structure cannot be determined at this stage, Allahabad High Court said as it declined the challenge by mosque that the suits filed by Hindus are barred by Places of Worship Act

title: India’s gig internet workforce may reach 17-21 million by 2030, says Bengaluru firm Redseer
content: Across delivery, ride-hailing and home services, full-time gig workers earn, on average, up to 2.5 times the monthly net income of workers in comparable formal and informal occupations

title: Centre concedes to States’ demands on Higher Education Bill
content: Responding to concerns raised by NDA allies TDP and NPP, the Centre agrees to amend at least two clauses of the legislation, leaving State universities out of certain aspects of the regulatory structure

title: S. Janaki (1938-2026) — A collection recalling the singer's pan-India career
content: S. Janaki won the National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for “Senthoora Poove” from 16 Vayathinile, the debut directorial venture of Bharathiraja, who passed away last month

title: Three men sentenced to jail in separate cases of raping, abducting, and harassing minor girl
content: In a series of decisive judgments, a special court in Kalaburagi handed a 25-year jail term to a prime accused for rape, while sentencing two other youths to three years of imprisonment each for abducting and sexually harassing the same 12-year-old survivor.

title: Skyroot makes history! 6 payloads put into low-Earth orbit aboard pvt rocket Vikram-1
content: Skyroot Aerospace launched India's first privately developed orbital rocket, Vikram-1.  The vehicle successfully placed four technology demonstration satellites into low-Earth orbit.  This launch marks a significant milestone in opening India's space sector to private companies.  Vikram-1 utilized advanced carbon-composite structures and a restartable liquid-fuelled module.  The mission validates India's capabilities in private orbital launch vehicle development.

title: 'Messi a source of pride for La Masia': Barcelona make big statement ahead of final
content: Laporta is in New York ahead of Sunday's final at New York/New Jersey Stadium, where current and former Barcelona players will feature on both sides. Spain's squad includes eight Barcelona players, while Messi will captain Argentina.

title: Apple is banning plumbers, electricians from advertising on Apple Maps
content: Apple Maps has updated its advertising policies, prohibiting home service providers.  Cryptocurrency ATMs and bail bond services are also banned from advertising.  Medical services will undergo case-by-case evaluation for advertising eligibility.  These new rules took effect on July fourteenth, impacting future ad placements.  Apple Maps advertisements are preparing for a summer launch in North America.

title: PV Sindhu breaks Chen Yufei jinx, becomes first Indian woman to ...
content: PV Sindhu has reached the Japan Open 2026 final, a historic achievement for Indian badminton. She advanced after her opponent Chen Yufei retired due to injury during their match. Sindhu secured her first win against Chen since 2019, ending a significant losing streak. This marks her return to a BWF Tour title match after over two years. She will now face Akane Yamaguchi for the championship title.

title: New money? India's currency might soon get a plastic makeover
content: India's Reserve Bank is exploring polymer banknotes for enhanced durability. Manufacturers are invited to bid for specialized plastic substrate materials. Polymer notes offer greater resistance to moisture and dirt than paper. This move aims to reduce currency replacement and printing costs. Advanced security features will also be incorporated into the new notes.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Mom spends $17K turning basement into hockey rink for her sons
content: Amanda Vernaci created a hockey rink in her basement over three months.  This ambitious DIY project cost approximately seventeen thousand dollars for her family.  She began her DIY journey in a rental home, sharing budget-friendly projects.  Her skills developed further while pregnant, teaching herself to use tools.  The basement rink was built around her sons' passion for the sport

title: Days after Atlas warning, robot dispute shuts down a car factory
content: Hyundai workers in South Korea have initiated a partial strike over automation concerns.  The union demands safeguards before Boston Dynamics' Atlas robot deployment.  Negotiations are ongoing regarding job security and AI integration into factories.  This action marks the auto industry's first factory shutdown due to automation.  The strike could significantly impact Hyundai's vehicle production and sales figures.

title: Buying a plot or a house? Here's which loan is right for you
content: Homebuyers are faced with a decision between land loans and home loans when it comes to acquiring property. Home loans cater to those looking to finance ready homes or their construction, while land loans specifically support the purchase of plots. Though home loans provide higher financing limits and longer repayment periods, land loans usually demand larger down payments and may accompany steeper interest rates.

title: 'He was everything we dream of becoming': Gavaskar's moving tribute to Garry Sobers
content: Widely regarded as cricket's greatest all-rounder, Sobers died at his home in Barbados, 11 days before his 90th birthday. During a career spanning two decades, he set new standards with his batting, bowling and fielding.

title: Plain clothes, white sheets, 3 layers: How Delhi Police planned Wangchuk's shift to hospital
content: Delhi Police conducted a pre-planned operation to remove Sonam Wangchuk from Jantar Mantar.  The activist was then shifted to Safdarjung Hospital due to his health.  Police cited a Delhi High Court order and medical advice for the action.  Protesters continued their demonstration after Wangchuk's removal from the site.  The operation aimed to minimize resistance and ensure a clear route.

title: Nolan Wells update: Eerie Horn Island discovery detail raises fresh questions
content: Rescuer Brian Trascher says the strong currents and sudden underwater drop-off at Horn Island’s northwest tip may explain how Nolan Wells died. Authorities are still waiting for autopsy results and say they have found no evidence of foul play. Meanwhile, Nolan’s family is pursuing an independent investigation, and the case will also be presented to a grand jury.

title: Why Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are delaying their honeymoon despite recent wedding
content: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift have spent their first weeks of married life moving between Montana, California, and Florida rather than on a traditional honeymoon. The couple attended a friend's wedding, trained, and stayed largely out of public view. Sources say Travis's July 28 Chiefs training camp deadline is the primary factor shaping whatever private trip they may still plan. Insiders report the couple has kept the destination secret even from close family, with plans to share details only once they are on the way back.

title: Blake Lively left heartbroken as Gigi Hadid reportedly deepens Taylor Swift rift
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce kept the drama away from their New York City wedding as they invited pretty much everyone from Hollywood and the NFL but Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, sparking chaos among many. While this has sparked a mixed reaction among fans, it seems supermodel Gigi Hadid might have made things worse for Blake Lively.

title: 'It's just insane': Messi reacts to viral photo with baby Yamal ahead of final
content: "That picture with Lamine, now we are facing in a final after a photo was taken of when he was a baby," Messi told seven-time Super Bowl winner Tom Brady at Fanatics Fest on Friday. "It's just insane."

title: Ranbir Kapoor, Sai Pallavi spotted at airport ahead of 'Ramayana' event
content: The promotional campaign for Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana has officially begun today. Actors Ranbir Kapoor and Sai Pallavi departed for New Delhi for the launch event. The grand event will feature an exclusive preview of the film's first trailer. This ambitious mythological spectacle is set for a Diwali 2026 release. The team will also promote the film at Comic-Con and TIFF.

title: Wangchuk opposed hospitalisation a day before; CJP shares video
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk opposed his hospitalization, stating his fast was self-chosen.  Delhi Police moved him to Safdarjung Hospital citing health concerns and court orders.  The Cockroach Janta Party shared a video showing Wangchuk's objection to hospitalization efforts.  Wangchuk has been on an indefinite hunger strike since June 28 supporting the NEET protest.  His wife also questioned the decision to hospitalize him without consent.

title: After 'Dhurandhar', Aditya Dhar eyes grand Lachit Borphukan biopic
content: Aditya Dhar could soon be taking on another high-profile project. Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has revealed that he has approached the director to helm a biopic on legendary Ahom commander Lachit Borphukan.

title: 'Cricket has lost one of its greatest': Virat Kohli mourns Garry Sobers' death
content: Kohli took to X to express his condolences and praised Sobers' impact on the game. "Cricket has lost one of its greatest. Rest in peace, Sir Garfield Sobers. Your legacy will inspire generations," Kohli wrote.

title: 'Just like Messi for Argentina': Kaif makes strong case for Rohit, Kohli in 2027 WC
content: Mohammed Kaif believes Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli are essential for India's 2027 World Cup campaign. He stressed their experience will be vital for challenging conditions in South Africa. Kaif compared their importance to Lionel Messi's impact on his football team. Experienced players offer guidance and create opportunities for younger teammates. India will need these seasoned cricketers under captain Shubman Gill.

title: Blood oozing from nose, UP cop found dead inside car
content: A sub-inspector was found dead inside his locked car in Para on Friday.  He had been out of contact with colleagues since Thursday afternoon.  Police traced his mobile phone to the Budheshwar-Mohan road location.  Doctors declared him dead after he was shifted to a hospital.  Forensic examination and post-mortem will establish the exact cause of death.

title: CDC finds shredded lettuce behind Taco Bell-linked cyclospora outbreak
content: US health officials identified Taco Bell's shredded lettuce as the source of a Cyclospora outbreak. This parasitic infection sickened over 1,600 people and led to 94 hospitalizations across five states. Investigations traced the contaminated lettuce through the supply chain to a farm in central Mexico. Taylor Farms voluntarily removed its iceberg lettuce from the US market and initiated a recall.

title: 'They rigged election, & I got FIFA WC': Trump praises Balogun decision, repeats 2020 claim
content: Trump made the remarks during a reception at Trump Tower before a FIFA Council meeting on Friday, two days ahead of Sunday's World Cup final.

title: Eye on Russia: UK's Storm Fighter, Germany’s Tranche‑4 Eurofighters signal new era of air defence
content: Europe is increasing defense spending to counter Russian aggression and enhance collective security.  The United Kingdom is developing its Storm Fighter drone program for future combat operations. Germany is modernizing its air force by fielding new Tranche 4 Eurofighter aircraft. These initiatives reflect a broader European push for military capability upgrades.  Nations are investing in autonomous systems and updating existing fighter fleets.

title: Shatrughan Sinha backs Sonakshi Sinha for supporting Sonam Wangchuk, his team thanks her
content: Veteran actor and politician Shatrughan Sinha has publicly praised daughter Sonakshi Sinha for speaking in support of activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on an indefinite hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak issue. Following the support from Sonakshi Sinha and actor Omi Vaidya, Sonam Wangchuk's team responded on behalf of the activist, expressing gratitude for their solidarity.

title: 'Promoting love jihad': Aamir Khan gets 'choke the breath' threat from Lawrence Bishnoi gang
content: The Lawrence Bishnoi gang has issued death threats against Bollywood actor Aamir Khan. Gangsters Aarzoo Bishnoi and Tyson Bishnoi made the threats via social media and audio clips. The group also warned politicians shielding alleged culprits in a Rajasthan case. They paid tribute to deceased associates, vowing retaliation for their sacrifice. The gang claims Khan and others promote 'love jihad' against their culture.

title: 'Historic new frontier': PM Modi hails 'talent of youth' ahead of Vikram-1 launch
content: Skyroot Aerospace prepares for the maiden orbital launch of Vikram-1 today.  Prime Minister Modi hailed this as a historic new frontier for India.  This mission marks the first time a private Indian company attempts an orbital launch.  Vikram-1 is a four-stage rocket designed for rapid launch services.  The launch showcases India's growing private space sector capabilities.

title: After Andhra, Covid-19 resurfaces in Maharashtra after 3 years
content: Maharashtra has seen a rise in Covid-19 cases, mostly mild, over recent days.  Experts note the virus now follows influenza-like patterns with periodic spikes.  Most positive cases were detected during physician-advised investigations or pre-operative assessments.  This recent uptick is the first noticeable increase in Covid cases since around 2023.  Hospitals are also observing increased cases of influenza and other infections.

title: Priyanka Chopra's first images show Mandakini avatar; film set for Aug 7, 2027
content: Priyanka Chopra rings in her birthday with stunning new looks from the upcoming film Varanasi. With birthday wishes pouring in from industry stalwarts like Mahesh Babu and Prithviraj Sukumar, this celebration is truly special. Marking her much-anticipated return to Indian cinema after her role in The Sky Is Pink (2019), Varanasi is set to hit theaters on August 7, 2027.

title: 'Tie nuptial knot to her body': How techie's suicide case turned into demand for posthumous marriage
content: Tension arose in Ponduru village after a woman's family brought her body to her boyfriend's residence. They demanded he marry her corpse before her final rites could be performed. The woman, Keerthi, died by suicide after her boyfriend reportedly refused to marry her. Her family claimed this was her dying wish before her cremation. The family eventually conducted the cremation after Sai's family agreed to the rites.

title: 'Do we need Kohli & Rohit?': Ashwin's blunt take on 2027 World Cup plans
content: Ravichandran Ashwin questions the timing of speculation about Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's ODI futures. He believes selectors should have communicated any transition plans much earlier. Ashwin stated that the experienced duo's presence is still needed for upcoming tours. Honest communication is essential when managing senior players' careers. Such discussions are surfacing late, with the next World Cup approaching.

title: 46 yrs after SLV-3, Skyroot’s Vikram-1 set to mark new chapter in private space journey
content: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1, India's first private orbital rocket, is scheduled for launch. This launch occurs 46 years after ISRO's SLV-3 mission from Sriharikota. The Vikram-1 rocket is 22 meters tall and built with advanced composite structures. This maiden orbital test flight aims to gather crucial flight data for future development. The mission marks a significant milestone for India's burgeoning private space sector.

title: Lamine Yamal separated from Spain squad ahead of final against Argentina
content: Spain have eased concerns over Lamine Yamal's fitness after the teenager trained separately ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup final against Argentina. The RFEF and coach Luis de la Fuente insist the session was precautionary, with Pedro Porro also on an individual programme. Both players are expected to be fit as Spain chase a fourth World Cup title on Sunday.

title: Rickshaw puller's grandson from Bihar village cracks NEET UG, shares success tips
content: Aditya from Ratnauli village in Muzaffarpur has emerged as a symbol of determination after securing AIR 946 in NEET UG, becoming the first student from his village to crack the medical entrance exam. His grandfather Saukhi Sahni was a former rickshaw puller, while his father works in a private job. The article highlights Aditya’s achievement along with other Muzaffarpur students who secured notable NEET-UG ranks through dedicated preparation.

title: 'The Odyssey' box office day 1: Christopher Nolan's epic scores Rs 20 crore debut
content: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' registered a strong opening at the Indian box office. The film earned Rs 20.76 crore on its opening day. The English version led collections, contributing Rs 13.75 crore net. Premium formats like IMAX screenings significantly boosted the opening day numbers. The film also achieved strong advance bookings, ranking among top Hollywood pre-sales.

title: "You Made India Proud": PM Modi Dials In To Join Vikram-1 Launch Celebrations
content: Commending the young Skyroot team, the Prime Minister said they had shown what India's youth are capable of

content: The mission, called Aagaman, marks the arrival of India's private sector in the global launch business.

title: India's 1st Geothermal Wells Commissioned At 14,000 Feet In Ladakh
content: Established at an altitude of 14,000 feet, the two deep wells are expected to pave the way for a one-megawatt geothermal power plant in Ladakh.

title: "3...2...1...Lift Off": Moment India's 1st Private Space Rocket Took Off. Watch
content: Vikram-1 is India's longest monolithic carbon composite rocket stage, which showcases advances in indigenous composite manufacturing and aerospace engineering.

title: Punjab Cop Who Extorted Rs 4 Crore From Family And Was Wanted By US Arrested
content: Punjab police officer Gurinderjit Singh Nagra arrested over US racketeering and extortion allegations.

title: Mars, Moon, Sun, Sea: India's Space Achievements As Vikram-1 Lifts Off
content: Skyroot Aerospace's plans to launch the country's first privately developed orbital-class rocket before August 4 mark the next major step for the growing Indian space industry.

title: Iran Claims 2 Oil Tankers Exploded After Hitting Mines In Hormuz, US Denies
content: Responding to the claim, the US Central Command (CENTCOM) said, "Like most IRGC claims, this is false," on X.

title: 'Good Decision': Trump On FIFA Chief Reversing US Player's Suspension
content: Folarin Balogun's case became a flashpoint in the tournament, generating widespread criticism of FIFA's leadership and the US president.

title: "Won't Tolerate This Man": Lawrence Bishnoi Gang Threatens Aamir Khan
content: The threats were allegedly issued by Lawrence Bishnoi's brother, Arzu Bishnoi, and another person identified as Tyson Bishnoi.

title: Civilian Clothes, White Sheets: How Cops Took Sonam Wangchuk From Protest Site
content: Sonam Wangchuk has been taken to the Safdarjung Hospital, where he has been admitted to the emergency ward.

title: "To Minimise Difficulties": India To Engage With US On Revised Visa Policies
content: India is communicating with US authorities to ease issues from new visa rules affecting Indian students and visitors.

title: "Zero To Hero": Space Regulator Chief On Skyroot's Historic Vikram-1 Launch
content: Speaking to NDTV, IN-SPACe Chairman Dr Pawan Goenka described Vikram-1 as a milestone not just for Skyroot Aerospace but also for India and the global space industry.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Removed From Jantar Mantar: A Timeline Of His Hunger Strike
content: Sonam Wangchuk and his supporters had been protesting against Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan for nearly a month.

title: India To Launch 1st Private Space Rocket With PM Modi's "Vande Mataram" Note
content: As India's private space sector prepares for another defining moment, a special message from Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to ride into space aboard Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Iran Claims 2 Oil Tankers Exploded After Hitting Mines In Hormuz, US Denies
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".

Be it how-tos or the latest happenings in AI, cybersecurity, personal gadgets, platforms like WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook and more; TOI Tech Desk brings the news with accuracy and authenticity. title: Wangchuk shifted to Safdarjung hospital after 20 days of indefinite fast
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More
